title: सूर्य चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सूर्य चालीसा ॥

॥ दोहा ॥
कनक बदन कुण्डल मकर, मुकुट किरीट विराज।
दिव्य दृष्टि अरुणो ज्योति, भानु शंख चक्र गदा विराज॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय जय जय रवि देव दयाला।
करत सदा भक्तन प्रतिपाला॥
अरुण जुता रथ सात घोड़ा।
तीनों लोक करत हैं दौड़ा॥
गंगा यमुना गोदावरी।
सरस्वती ये धाराधरी॥
मकर राशि में जब रवि आवे।
सब पुण्य तीरथ तब हो जावे॥
द्वादश रूप धरे रवि देवा।
अरुणतेज करे सब सेवा॥
इन्द्र चन्द्र यम वरुण कुबेरा।
शनि बुध शुक्र गुरू सब तेरा॥
लंकापति निशिचर जब आयो।
तब प्रभु रामहिं मन्त्र बतायो॥
आदित्य हृदय जो कोई गावे।
रिपु पर जय सुख सम्पद पावे॥
ग्रह बृहस्पति शनि जब आवें।
तब तू भक्तन को बचावें॥
राम कृष्ण रूप तू धारी।
कृपा कर प्रभु भक्त सुखारी॥
गायत्री मन्त्र जो जपावे।
मन वांछित फल सोई पावे॥
तीनों लोक तिमिर जब छावे।
तब तेरी ज्योति अँधेर मिटावे॥
भोर होत कमल सब फूले।
तेरे तेज से कली न भूले॥
कमल नयन मधुर मुस्कावे।
अरुण प्रकाश धरा पर आवे॥
तेरे तेज बिना सब सूना।
ज्ञान नयन सब रहत अजूना॥
सूर्य किरण जो पानी पावे।
गंगा जमुन ते पावन हो जावे॥
सब ग्रह तव आज्ञाकारी।
करत सदा तव चाकरी सारी॥
चन्द्रमा ज्योति तेरे तेज से।
तारे जगमग होत सुवेश से॥
उत्तर दक्षिण अयन तेरा।
ऋतु वसन्त ग्रीष्म रवि मेरा॥
जो जन शनिवार व्रत पावे।
रोग दोष तन सब मिट जावे॥
सूर्य ग्रहण जब होत जगत में।
तब तू छिपत न दिखत रत में॥
सहस्र नाम तेरे गुण गावें।
भानु तेज सबही मन भावें॥
सब जीवन के पालनहारी।
भवसागर से करो उबारी॥
मकर संक्रान्ति पर्व तुम्हारा।
पूजें सब संसार तुम्हारा॥
छठ पूजा में सबसे पहले।
तुमको अर्घ्य चढ़ावें सहले॥
रथ सप्तमी पर्व अति प्यारा।
करत सदा तव ध्यान हमारा॥
गंगा यमुना सरयू नर्मदा।
सब पावन तेरी किरणों से सदा॥
जो नित प्रति सूर्य चालीसा गावे।
सो सात जनम का पाप नसावे॥
रोग शोक दरिद्र मिट जावे।
मन वांछित फल सो जन पावे॥
प्रातः काल उठ जो यह पढ़ई।
ताके सब सुख सम्पत्ति बढ़ई॥
सब विघ्न ताके मिट जावें।
मनोवांछित फल सो सब पावें॥
जन्म जन्म का दुख सब नासे।
नित्य पाठ करे मन उल्लासे॥
सूर्य चालीसा जो पढ़े सुनावे।
सो भवसागर पार तर जावे॥
नेत्र रोग हरे सब पीरा।
पाठ करे सो पावे धीरा॥
सब कामना हृदय की पूरे।
दूर करे सब संकट क्रूरे॥
ताप तीनों सब मिट जावें।
सूर्य देव की शरण जो आवें॥
कर जोरि विनय जो आवे।
सो जन मनवांछित फल पावे॥
कर जोरि प्रभु पद पर माथा।
सदा रहे प्रभु जन के साथा॥

॥ दोहा ॥
पाठ करे चालीसा जो, भानु देव मतिमान।
ताकी सदा विजय रहे, मिटें सकल दुख हान॥

॥ इति श्री सूर्य चालीसा सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Sūrya Chālīsā ||

|| Dohā ||
Kanaka badana kuṇḍala makara, mukuṭa kirīṭa virāja.
Divya dṛṣṭi aruṇo jyoti, bhānu śaṅkha chakra gadā virāja.

|| Chaupāī ||
Jaya jaya jaya Ravi deva dayālā.
Karata sadā bhaktana pratipālā.
Aruṇa jutā ratha sāta ghoṛā.
Tīnoṁ loka karata haiṁ dauṛā.
Gaṅgā Yamunā Godāvarī.
Sarasvatī ye dhārādharī.
Makara rāśi meṁ jaba Ravi āve.
Saba puṇya tīratha taba ho jāve.
Dvādaśa rūpa dhare Ravi devā.
Aruṇateja kare saba sevā.
Indra Chandra Yama Varuṇa Kuberā.
Śani Budha Śukra Gurū saba terā.
Laṅkāpati niśichara jaba āyo.
Taba Prabhu Rāmahiṁ mantra batāyo.
Āditya hṛdaya jo koī gāve.
Ripu para jaya sukha sampada pāve.
Graha Bṛhaspati Śani jaba āveṁ.
Taba tū bhaktana ko bachāveṁ.
Rāma Kṛṣṇa rūpa tū dhārī.
Kṛpā kara Prabhu bhakta sukhārī.
Gāyatrī mantra jo japāve.
Mana vāñchhita phala soī pāve.
Tīnoṁ loka timira jaba chhāve.
Taba terī jyoti aṁdhera miṭāve.
Bhora hota kamala saba phūle.
Tere teja se kalī na bhūle.
Kamala nayana madhura muskāve.
Aruṇa prakāśa dharā para āve.
Tere teja binā saba sūnā.
Jñāna nayana saba rahata ajūnā.
Sūrya kiraṇa jo pānī pāve.
Gaṅgā Jamuna te pāvana ho jāve.
Saba graha tava ājñākārī.
Karata sadā tava chākarī sārī.
Chandramā jyoti tere teja se.
Tāre jagamaga hota suveśa se.
Uttara dakṣiṇa ayana terā.
Ṛtu vasanta grīṣma Ravi merā.
Jo jana Śanivāra vrata pāve.
Roga doṣa tana saba miṭa jāve.
Sūrya grahaṇa jaba hota jagata meṁ.
Taba tū chhipata na dikhata rata meṁ.
Sahasra nāma tere guṇa gāveṁ.
Bhānu teja sabahī mana bhāveṁ.
Saba jīvana ke pālanahārī.
Bhavasāgara se karo ubārī.
Makara Saṅkrānti parva tumhārā.
Pūjeṁ saba saṁsāra tumhārā.
Chhaṭha Pūjā meṁ sabase pahale.
Tumako arghya chaṛhāveṁ sahale.
Ratha Saptamī parva ati pyārā.
Karata sadā tava dhyāna hamārā.
Gaṅgā Yamunā Sarayū Narmadā.
Saba pāvana terī kiraṇoṁ se sadā.
Jo nita prati Sūrya Chālīsā gāve.
So sāta janama kā pāpa nasāve.
Roga śoka daridra miṭa jāve.
Mana vāñchhita phala so jana pāve.
Prātaḥ kāla uṭha jo yaha paṛhaī.
Tāke saba sukha sampatti baṛhaī.
Saba vighna tāke miṭa jāveṁ.
Manovāñchhita phala so saba pāveṁ.
Janma janma kā dukha saba nāse.
Nitya pāṭha kare mana ullāse.
Sūrya Chālīsā jo paṛhe sunāve.
So bhavasāgara pāra tara jāve.
Netra roga hare saba pīrā.
Pāṭha kare so pāve dhīrā.
Saba kāmanā hṛdaya kī pūre.
Dūra kare saba saṅkaṭa krūre.
Tāpa tīnoṁ saba miṭa jāveṁ.
Sūrya deva kī śaraṇa jo āveṁ.
Kara joṛi vinaya jo āve.
So jana manavāñchhita phala pāve.
Kara joṛi Prabhu pada para māthā.
Sadā rahe Prabhu jana ke sāthā.

|| Dohā ||
Pāṭha kare Chālīsā jo, Bhānu deva matimāna.
Tākī sadā vijaya rahe, miṭeṁ sakala dukha hāna.

|| Iti Shrī Sūrya Chālīsā sampūrṇa ||
hindiMeaning: सोने के समान शरीर वाले, मकर कुण्डल धारण करने वाले, मुकुट और किरीट से विराजमान, दिव्य दृष्टि वाले, अरुण ज्योति से युक्त, शंख-चक्र-गदा धारी सूर्य भगवान की जय हो। हे दयालु सूर्यदेव! आप सदा अपने भक्तों की रक्षा करते हैं। अरुण सारथी, सात घोड़ों वाले रथ पर बैठकर तीनों लोकों में भ्रमण करते हैं।
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of sins accumulated over seven lifetimes
Associated in tradition with eye diseases and all physical ailments
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty, grief, and suffering
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires of the heart
Invokes victory and prosperity in life
Traditionally invoked for protection from planetary afflictions
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Surya Chalisa — सूर्य चालीसा — is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn offered to Sun God Surya. Attributed to Traditional Hindu Prayer, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to cure eye diseases and all physical ailments and grant victory and prosperity in life. It is also said to destroy sins accumulated over seven lifetimes. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Makar Sankranti. With 40 verses and a reading time of about 12 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Surya Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Surya Chalisa and what is its purpose?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Chalisa (सूर्य चालीसा) is a 40-verse devotional hymn (chalisa = forty chaupais) in Awadhi-Hindi addressed to Bhagavan Surya — the Sun God — the visible deity (pratyaksha-devata) of Hindu worship and the source of life, light, health and time itself. The composition opens with framing dohas describing Surya's golden body, makara-kundalas, crown and conch-discus-mace, then flows into 40 chaupais that praise his seven-horse chariot driven by Aruna, his daily journey across the three worlds, his twelve forms (Dwadasha Aditya), and his lordship over all the planets. The phalashruti promises destruction of seven lifetimes of sins, removal of disease (especially of the eyes), removal of poverty and suffering, and fulfilment of all desires. (Source: traditional Hindi-Awadhi devotional chalisa tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Surya Chalisa be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Chalisa is most powerful when chanted at sunrise (the supreme Surya muhurta) on Sunday (Ravivar — Surya's principal day), with the devotee facing east, after offering arghya (water libation) to the rising sun. Major festival days are Makar Sankranti (Surya enters Capricorn, mid-January, the supreme Surya parva), Ratha Saptami (Magha Shukla Saptami — celebrating Surya's chariot turning northwards, also called Surya Jayanti), Chhath Puja (Kartik Shukla Shashti in Bihar/UP/Jharkhand — the four-day vrat to Surya and Chhathi Maiya), every Sankranti (12 solar transits a year), during Surya Grahana, and throughout the month of Magha. Daily morning recitation is especially recommended for those with afflicted Sun in horoscope. (Sources: Drikpanchang Surya parva calendar; traditional Hindi panchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Surya Chalisa promise according to its phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Surya Chalisa explicitly promises: (1) saat janam ka paap nasave — destruction of sins accumulated over seven lifetimes, (2) rog shok daridra mit jave — removal of disease, grief and poverty, (3) sukha sampatti badhayi — increase of happiness and wealth, (4) all obstacles destroyed (sab vighna mit javen), (5) netra roga hare sab pira — removal of all eye diseases and physical pain, (6) tap tinon mit javen — removal of the three taapas (adhyatmika, adhibhautika, adhidaivika sufferings), and (7) bhavasagara paar — crossing the ocean of worldly existence. Special benefits are attributed to recitation for victory in disputes, planetary peace for those with adverse Sun, and for general health, vitality and family prestige. (Source: Surya Chalisa phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Surya Chalisa different from the Aditya Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Both prayers are addressed to Surya but differ in scripture, language, length and purpose. The Aditya Hridayam is a 31-verse Sanskrit stotra from the Valmiki Ramayana (Yuddha Kanda) — taught by sage Agastya to Lord Rama on the battlefield before his final duel with Ravana — promising victory, removal of enemies and royal Vedic dignity. The Surya Chalisa is a 40-verse Hindi-Awadhi popular devotional chalisa (modelled on the Hanuman Chalisa pattern) accessible to non-Sanskrit devotees and beloved across north India for daily household worship. The Aditya Hridayam is the Vedic-Puranic root prayer; the Chalisa is the popular bhakti expression. Devotees often recite both — the Chalisa daily and the Hridayam on Sundays and festival days. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana Yuddha Kanda 105; traditional Surya bhakti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Can the Surya Chalisa be used as a remedy for adverse Sun in astrology?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. In Vedic astrology (Jyotisha) the Sun rules confidence, vitality, government favour, father, eyes, heart, bones and the soul (atmakaraka). When Surya is afflicted in a horoscope (debilitated in Libra, combust, in malefic conjunctions, in dasha of Surya without strength, or in Sade Sati patterns), traditional remedies prescribed by jyotishis include: chanting the Surya Chalisa daily at sunrise facing east, reciting the Aditya Hridayam on Sundays, offering arghya with red flowers and gur, wearing a ruby (manikya) gemstone after consultation, donating wheat-jaggery-copper on Sundays, observing Sunday vrat, and visiting a Surya temple (Konark, Modhera, Suryanar Koil) on Ratha Saptami. The Chalisa is the most accessible daily remedy. (Sources: classical Jyotisha graha-shanti tradition; traditional Hindi astrology manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: Who are the twelve Adityas mentioned in Surya tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dwadasha Adityas (twelve solar forms) are twelve aspects of Surya, one for each month of the year, named in the Vishnu Purana, Markandeya Purana and Bhagavata Purana: (1) Mitra, (2) Ravi, (3) Surya, (4) Bhanu, (5) Khaga, (6) Pushan, (7) Hiranyagarbha, (8) Marichi, (9) Aditya, (10) Savitr, (11) Arka, and (12) Bhaskara. Each Aditya governs one solar month and represents a distinct aspect — Mitra the friend of all beings, Pushan the nourisher, Savitr the impeller (the Gayatri's deity), Bhaskara the maker of light, etc. The twelve names are the basis of the famous Surya Namaskar 12-mantra invocation (Om Mitraya Namah, Om Ravaye Namah, etc.) recited with the twelve postures. The Surya Chalisa references this twelvefold form in the verse Dwadasha rupa dhare Ravi deva. (Source: Vishnu Purana Book 2; Markandeya Purana.)
